ruleForward: A customer can place many orders.
ruleReverse: Each order must be placed by exactly one customer.
oralCue: customer_id belongs in Order because each order is on the many side and requires one customer.

ruleForward: A customer may have many addresses on file.
ruleReverse: Each address must belong to exactly one customer.
oralCue: customer_id belongs in Address because many addresses can belong to one customer.

ruleForward: A zipcode can be referenced by many addresses.
ruleReverse: Each address must reference exactly one zipcode.
oralCue: zipcode_id belongs in Address because each address uses one zipcode and a zipcode can be reused.

ruleForward: A customer may enroll in at most one loyalty program.
ruleReverse: Each loyalty program record must belong to exactly one customer.
oralCue: customer_id belongs in LoyaltyProgram because loyalty enrollment is optional for Customer but each loyalty record needs its customer.

ruleForward: An order can contain many products through the OrderLine associative entity.
ruleReverse: A product can appear in many orders through the OrderLine associative entity.
oralCue: The M:N relationship is resolved by OrderLine, which carries order_id and product_id plus quantity and unit_price.

ruleForward: An order must contain at least one order line.
ruleReverse: Each order line must belong to exactly one order.
oralCue: order_id is part of OrderLine because each line identifies the order it belongs to.

ruleForward: A product can be referenced in many order lines.
ruleReverse: Each order line must reference exactly one product.
oralCue: product_id is part of OrderLine because each line identifies the product being sold.

ruleForward: An order can have many payments.
ruleReverse: Each payment must be associated with exactly one order.
oralCue: order_id belongs in Payment because split payments create many payments for one order.

ruleForward: A payment method can be used across many payments.
ruleReverse: Each payment must use exactly one payment method.
oralCue: payment_method_id belongs in Payment because each payment chooses one method.

ruleForward: A gift card is a specialized subtype of payment method.
ruleReverse: Each gift card record must correspond to exactly one payment method record.
oralCue: GiftCard uses payment_method_id as both PK and FK to specialize PaymentMethod.

ruleForward: A store location can fulfill many orders.
ruleReverse: Each order must be fulfilled at exactly one store location.
oralCue: store_location_id belongs in Order because each order is fulfilled by one store.

ruleForward: A store location can generate many sales reports.
ruleReverse: Each sales report must be associated with exactly one store location.
oralCue: store_location_id belongs in SalesReport because each report is generated for one store.

ruleForward: A product category can contain many products.
ruleReverse: Each product must belong to exactly one product category.
oralCue: product_category_id belongs in Product because Product is the many side.

ruleForward: A seasonal item is a subtype of product.
ruleReverse: Each seasonal item must correspond to exactly one product, but not every product is a seasonal item.
oralCue: SeasonalItem uses product_id as both PK and FK to specialize Product.

ruleForward: A product can be featured in many promotions through the ProductPromotion associative entity.
ruleReverse: A promotion can apply to many products through the ProductPromotion associative entity.
oralCue: ProductPromotion resolves the M:N relationship and stores featured_flag and display_order.

ruleForward: A promotion can have many discounts.
ruleReverse: Each discount must be associated with exactly one promotion.
oralCue: promotion_id belongs in Discount because discounts are the many side under one promotion.

ruleForward: A customer can write many reviews.
ruleReverse: Each review must be written by exactly one customer.
oralCue: customer_id belongs in Review because each review has one author.

ruleForward: A product can receive many reviews.
ruleReverse: Each review must be associated with exactly one product.
oralCue: product_id belongs in Review because each review is about one product.

ruleForward: An ingredient can have many inventory records across different store locations.
ruleReverse: Each inventory record must reference exactly one ingredient.
oralCue: ingredient_id belongs in Inventory because one ingredient can be tracked at many stores.

ruleForward: A store location can hold many inventory records.
ruleReverse: Each inventory record must be held at exactly one store location.
oralCue: store_location_id belongs in Inventory because inventory is tracked per store.

ruleForward: A supplier can provide many ingredients through the SupplierIngredient associative entity.
ruleReverse: An ingredient can be sourced from many suppliers through the SupplierIngredient associative entity.
oralCue: SupplierIngredient resolves the M:N relationship and stores unit_cost and lead_time_days.

ruleForward: A product can be made with many ingredients through the Recipe associative entity.
ruleReverse: An ingredient can be used in many products through the Recipe associative entity.
oralCue: Recipe resolves the M:N relationship and stores quantity_required and unit.

ruleForward: A supplier can issue many invoices.
ruleReverse: Each invoice must be associated with exactly one supplier.
oralCue: supplier_id belongs in Invoice because each invoice comes from one supplier.

ruleForward: A department can incur many expenses.
ruleReverse: Each expense must be charged to exactly one department.
oralCue: department_id belongs in Expense because expenses are charged to one department.

ruleForward: A store location can have many expenses.
ruleReverse: Each expense must be incurred at exactly one store location.
oralCue: store_location_id belongs in Expense because each expense is tied to one store.

ruleForward: An ingredient can appear in many waste log entries.
ruleReverse: Each waste log entry must reference exactly one ingredient.
oralCue: ingredient_id belongs in WasteLog because each waste event records one ingredient.

ruleForward: A store location can have many waste log entries.
ruleReverse: Each waste log entry must be recorded at exactly one store location.
oralCue: store_location_id belongs in WasteLog because waste is recorded at one store.

ruleForward: A department can have many budgets over different fiscal years.
ruleReverse: Each budget must be allocated to exactly one department.
oralCue: department_id belongs in Budget because each budget is allocated to one department.

ruleForward: A store location can have many budgets.
ruleReverse: Each budget must be scoped to exactly one store location.
oralCue: store_location_id belongs in Budget because each budget is scoped to one store.

ruleForward: A role can be held by many employees.
ruleReverse: Each employee must be assigned exactly one role.
oralCue: role_id belongs in Employee because Employee is the many side.

ruleForward: A department can contain many employees.
ruleReverse: Each employee must belong to exactly one department.
oralCue: department_id belongs in Employee because each employee belongs to one department.

ruleForward: An employee can work many shifts.
ruleReverse: Each shift must be assigned to exactly one employee.
oralCue: employee_id belongs in Shift because one employee can have many shifts.

ruleForward: A store location can schedule many shifts.
ruleReverse: Each shift must take place at exactly one store location.
oralCue: store_location_id belongs in Shift because each shift happens at one store.

ruleForward: An order can be assigned to many employees through the OrderAssignment associative entity.
ruleReverse: An employee can be assigned to many orders through the OrderAssignment associative entity.
oralCue: OrderAssignment resolves the order-to-employee assignment and also references Shift.

ruleForward: A shift can cover many order assignments.
ruleReverse: Each order assignment must be linked to exactly one shift.
oralCue: shift_id belongs in OrderAssignment because each assignment is tied to one shift.

ruleForward: An employee can complete many training records.
ruleReverse: Each training record must belong to exactly one employee.
oralCue: employee_id belongs in Training because each training record is for one employee.

ruleForward: A role can have many training records associated with it.
ruleReverse: Each training record must be related to exactly one role.
oralCue: role_id belongs in Training because training is tied to the role being trained.

ruleForward: An event type can classify many events.
ruleReverse: Each event must be classified under exactly one event type.
oralCue: event_type_id belongs in Event because many events can share one type.

ruleForward: A customer can book many events.
ruleReverse: Each event must be booked by exactly one customer.
oralCue: customer_id belongs in Event because each event is booked by one customer.

ruleForward: An event can have many catering orders.
ruleReverse: Each catering order must be linked to exactly one event.
oralCue: event_id belongs in Catering because catering orders are tied to one event.

ruleForward: A store location can prepare many catering orders.
ruleReverse: Each catering order must be prepared at exactly one store location.
oralCue: store_location_id belongs in Catering because each catering order is prepared by one store.

ruleForward: A customer can place many catering orders.
ruleReverse: Each catering order must be placed by exactly one customer.
oralCue: customer_id belongs in Catering because each catering order is placed by one customer.

ruleForward: An order can have at most one delivery.
ruleReverse: Each delivery must be assigned to exactly one order.
oralCue: order_id belongs in Delivery because delivery is optional for an order but mandatory for a delivery record.

ruleForward: An employee can handle many deliveries.
ruleReverse: Each delivery must be handled by exactly one employee.
oralCue: employee_id belongs in Delivery because each delivery has one assigned employee.

ruleForward: An address can receive many deliveries.
ruleReverse: Each delivery must be sent to exactly one address.
oralCue: address_id belongs in Delivery because each delivery has one destination address.

ruleForward: A product must have exactly one nutritional info record.
ruleReverse: Each nutritional info record must belong to exactly one product.
oralCue: product_id belongs in NutritionalInfo because each nutritional record describes one product.
